Claims are limited to the official text and CRIA recommendations; mirrors, courses, and commission-news pages were rejected.

artifacts.sourceTitle: UFRJ atualiza diretrizes de integridade acadêmica e propõe recomendações sobre o uso de inteligência artificial
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: A Universidade Federal do Rio de Janeiro (UFRJ) tornou público dois documentos preliminares que orientam a comunidade acadêmica sobre o uso ético e responsável da inteligência artificial, em especial nas atividades de ensino e pesquisa.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The university announced two preliminary documents guiding ethical and responsible AI use in teaching and research.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow staged claim directly supported by official Portuguese source text; not marked human reviewed or canonical.

artifacts.claimText: UFRJ states that delegating monographs, dissertations, and theses to generative AI systems is considered academic dishonesty under the proposed integrity guidance.

citation.citationTitle: UFRJ atualiza diretrizes de integridade acadêmica e propõe recomendações sobre o uso de inteligência artificial
citation.publisher: Universidade Federal do Rio de Janeiro

artifacts.sourceTitle: UFRJ atualiza diretrizes de integridade acadêmica e propõe recomendações sobre o uso de inteligência artificial
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Simplesmente delegar a responsabilidade de elaboração desses trabalhos a terceiros ou a sistemas de Inteligência Artificial Generativa (IAGen) é considerado desonestidade acadêmica e pode resultar em sanções institucionais, inclusive a perda do título, de acordo com a proposta do documento.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Delegating academic works to generative AI is described as academic dishonesty and may lead to institutional sanctions under the proposed document.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: UFRJ atualiza diretrizes de integridade acadêmica e propõe recomendações sobre o uso de inteligência artificial
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: As diretrizes também destacam que o uso de ferramentas de IAGen pode auxiliar na preparação e elaboração desses trabalhos, incluindo exploração de temas de pesquisa e organização de ideias, mas a responsabilidade final sobre o conteúdo produzido deve permanecer vinculada à autoria humana.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Generative AI may assist preparation and organization, but final responsibility remains with human authorship.

artifacts.title: Universidade Federal do Rio de Janeiro staged AI policy crawl
artifacts.summary: Staged crawl found official UFRJ source text announcing preliminary academic-integrity revisions and CRIA recommendations on generative AI. Claims are limited to preliminary status, academic integrity, human responsibility, faculty course-level AI-use rules, and confidential-data risk warnings.
limitations: This staged bundle is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The UFRJ article describes two documents as preliminary; staged claims preserve that limitation and do not assert final binding university-wide policy beyond the source text.
The CRIA PDF is publicly linked by the official UFRJ article and was fetched without bypassing access controls.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
